div1.entryFree:
Bat, 1) the animal Vespertilio: Tp. I, 2, 340. V, 91. Mcb. III, 2, 40. IV, 1, 15. Hml. III, 4, 190 (who, that's but a queen, fair, sober, wise, would from a paddock, from a bat, a gib, such dear concernings hide?).
2) a heavy stick: so slides he down upon his grained b. Compl. 64. where go you with --s and clubs? Cor. I, 1, 57. Cor. I, 1, 57 whether your costard or my b. be the harder, Lr. IV, 6, 247 (Ff. ballow).
